nodes.label: liver
nodes.description: An exocrine gland which secretes bile and functions in metabolism of protein and carbohydrate and fat, synthesizes substances involved in the clotting of the blood, synthesizes vitamin A, detoxifies poisonous substances, stores glycogen, and breaks down worn-out erythrocytes[GO].

nodes.label: Pulsatile liver
nodes.description: A pulsating liver is characteristic of significant tricuspid regurgitation. Systolic pulsations of the liver in patients with primary severe tricuspid regurgitation are due to transmission of a prominent right atrial v wave.. Cardiac synchronous pulsation on palpation.

nodes.label: right artery of caudate lobe
nodes.description: An artery that branches from the right hepatic artery and supplies the lateral portion of the caudate lobe of the liver.

nodes.label: right lobe of thyroid gland
nodes.description: A lobe of thyroid gland that is in the right side of a thyroid gland.

nodes.label: right pulmonary artery, anomalous origin of, familial
left artery of caudate lobe
nodes.description: An artery that branches from the left hepatic artery and supplies the medial portion of the caudate lobe of the liver.

nodes.label: left lobe of thyroid gland
nodes.description: A lobe of thyroid gland that is in the left side of a thyroid gland.

nodes.label: liver subserosa
nodes.description: Subserosal tissue of liver. A zone of areolar connective tissue lying beneath the serous coat of the liver and distinguished with difficulty from the fibrous capsule of Glisson.

nodes.label: liver subserosa
nodes.description: Subserosal tissue of liver. A zone of areolar connective tissue lying beneath the serous coat of the liver and distinguished with difficulty from the fibrous capsule of Glisson.

nodes.label: subserosa
nodes.description: Contains nerves, blood vessels, lymphatics and lymph nodes. A layer of tissue between the muscularis and serosa.

nodes.label: liver serosa
nodes.description: Hepatic serous coat; peritoneal covering of the liver, enclosing almost all except for a triangular area on its posterior surface (the bare area of the liver) and a smaller area where the liver and gallbladder are in direct contact.

nodes.label: metabolic zone of liver
nodes.description: Any of the metabolic zones found in the liver. Liver tissue is heterogenous at the level of morphometry and histochemistry. This heterogeneity is linked to the position of a cell within the functional unit of the tissue, which, in turn, is related to the blood supply: cells located in the upstream zone differ from those in the downstream zone with respect to subcellular structures, key enzymes, translocators, and receptors and, therefore, have different metabolic capacities. This is the basis for metabolic zonation, a concept first proposed in mammals for carbohydrate metabolism and later expanded to include amino acid and ammonia metabolism, xenobiotic metabolism, protective metabolism, and formation of plasma proteins.

nodes.label: capillary system of liver
nodes.description: The smallest of the blood vessels, parts of the microcirculation, of the liver.

nodes.label: respiratory system capillary
nodes.description: A capillary that is part of a respiratory system [Automatically generated definition].

nodes.label: respiratory system capillary endothelium
nodes.description: An endothelium of capillary that is part of a respiratory system [Automatically generated definition].

nodes.label: subcapsular region of liver
nodes.description: Region of liver parenchyma immediately below the capsule. This region is frequently specified in pathology diagnoses.

nodes.label: subcapsular region of liver
nodes.description: Region of liver parenchyma immediately below the capsule. This region is frequently specified in pathology diagnoses.

nodes.label: Subcapsular cataract
nodes.description: A cataract that affects the region of the lens directly beneath the capsule of the lens.

nodes.label: Posterior subcapsular cataract
nodes.description: A type of cataract affecting the posterior pole of lens immediately adjacent to (beneath) the Lens capsule.

nodes.label: ventral liver lobe
nodes.description: Portion of the liver which develops on the fishs ventral side.

nodes.label: lobe of liver
nodes.description: Traditional gross anatomy divided the liver into four lobes based on surface features. The falciform ligament is visible on the front (anterior side) of the liver. This divides the liver into a left anatomical lobe, and a right anatomical lobe.

nodes.label: Abnormal glomerular capillary morphology
nodes.description: A structural anomaly of the capillary blood vessels in the renal glomerulus.

nodes.label: capillary lymphangioma
nodes.description: A lymphangioma that involves the capillary.

nodes.label: Triangular lunate bone
nodes.description: See Figure 3 of PMID:23616330.. A disruption of the greater and lesser arcs of the lunate bone, with the lunate having a triangular silhouette, know as the piece of pie sign. Often caused by falling on an outstretched hand, patients will have tenderness to palpation over the dorsum of the wrist and may have sensory deficits in the median nerve distribution.

nodes.label: uterine ligament neoplasm
nodes.description: A benign, borderline, or malignant neoplasm that affects the uterine ligaments. Representative examples include Wolffian adnexal tumor, papillary cystadenoma, and adenocarcinoma.

nodes.label: Congenital absence of portal vein
nodes.description: Anomaly where the intestinal and the splenic venous drainage bypass the liver and drain into systemic veins through other possible venous shunts.

nodes.label: capsule of liver
nodes.description: A collagenous capsule covering the external surface of the liver.

nodes.label: liver stroma
nodes.description: A stroma that is part of a liver.

nodes.label: Abnormal corneal stroma morphology
nodes.description: An abnormality of the stroma of cornea, also known as the substantia propria of cornea.

nodes.label: stroma-dominant and stroma-poor composite ganglioneuroblastoma
nodes.description: A neuroblastic tumor characterized by the presence of a ganglioneuroblastoma component and the formation of Schwannian stroma which is the predominant component of the tumor volume.

nodes.label: schwannian stroma-rich and stroma-poor composite ganglioneuroblastoma
nodes.description: A neuroblastic tumor characterized by the presence of a ganglioneuroblastoma component and the formation of Schwannian stroma which constitutes more than fifty-percent of the tumor volume.

nodes.label: liver blood vessel
nodes.description: A blood vessel that is part of a liver.

nodes.label: blood vessel
nodes.description: A vessel through which blood circulates in the body.

nodes.label: Vascular neoplasm
nodes.description: A benign or malignant neoplasm (tumor) originating in the vascular system.

nodes.label: blood vessel neoplasm
nodes.description: A neoplasm arising from arteries or veins.

nodes.label: benign blood vessel neoplasm
nodes.description: A benign neoplasm arising from arteries or veins.

nodes.label: liver reticuloendothelial system
nodes.description: A reticuloendothelial system that is part of a liver.

nodes.label: ligament of liver
nodes.description: A nonskeletal ligament that is part of a liver.

nodes.label: liver trabecula
nodes.description: A trabecula that is part of a liver.

nodes.label: vasculature of liver
nodes.description: A vasculature that is part of a liver.

nodes.label: Abnormal macular vasculature morphology
nodes.description: Any structural anomaly of the blood vessels of the macula.

nodes.label: papillary adenocarcinoma
nodes.description: A morphologic variant of adenocarcinoma. It is characterized by the presence of a papillary growth pattern. Representative examples include thyroid gland papillary carcinoma, invasive papillary breast carcinoma, and ovarian serous surface papillary adenocarcinoma.

nodes.label: papillary adenoma
nodes.description: An adenoma characterized by the presence of papillary epithelial patterns.

nodes.label: biliary ductule
nodes.description: One of the fine terminal elements of the bile duct system, leaving the portal canal, and pursuing a course at the periphery of a lobule of the liver[BTO]. the excretory ducts of the liver that connect the interlobular ductules to the right or left hepatic duct[MP].

nodes.label: intrahepatic bile duct
nodes.description: Passages within the liver for the conveyance of bile. Includes right and left hepatic ducts even though these may join outside the liver to form the common hepatic duct.

nodes.label: intrahepatic bile duct
nodes.description: Passages within the liver for the conveyance of bile. Includes right and left hepatic ducts even though these may join outside the liver to form the common hepatic duct.

nodes.label: hepatic sinusoid
nodes.description: Wide thin-walled blood vessels in the liver. In mammals they have neither veinous or arterial markers.

nodes.label: liver parenchyma
nodes.description: The functional units of the liver including the lobules.

nodes.label: liver right lobe parenchyma
nodes.description: A parenchyma that is part of a right lobe of liver [Automatically generated definition].

nodes.label: liver left lobe parenchyma
nodes.description: A parenchyma that is part of a left lobe of liver [Automatically generated definition].

nodes.label: lobe of liver
nodes.description: Traditional gross anatomy divided the liver into four lobes based on surface features. The falciform ligament is visible on the front (anterior side) of the liver. This divides the liver into a left anatomical lobe, and a right anatomical lobe.

nodes.label: right artery of caudate lobe — generic (L0)
left artery of caudate lobe — generic (L0)
intrahepatic branch of portal vein — generic (L0)
